title: DBlockedWalks

tex_statement.html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $C \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes and $\pi$ a walk in $G$: \[ \pi =\lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp. \]

  1. We say that the walk $\pi$ is $C$-$d$-blocked or $d$-blocked by $C$ to emphasize the use of the bidirected edges\footnote{The ŌĆ£dŌĆØ here stands for ŌĆ£directionalŌĆØ. d-separation was first only used for DAGs (without bidirected edges). For ADMGs it was then called ŌĆ£m-separationŌĆØ in \cite{Ric03}. But since the notion of m-separation is arguably the natural extension of d-separation and to avoid introducing more definitions, we will just call it d-separation as well, which will not create any ambiguity.} if either: \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
  2. $v_0 \in C$ or $v_n \in C$ or:
  3. there are two adjacent edges in $\pi$ of one of the following forms: \[\begin{array}{rccl} \text{ left chain: } & v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_k \in C,\\ \text{ right chain: } & v_{k-1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_k \in C,\\ \text{ fork: } & v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_k \in C,\\ \text{ collider: } & v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1} & \text{ with } & v_k \notin \Anc^G(C). \end{array}\]

\item We say that the walk $\pi$ is $C$-$d$-open if it is not $C$-$d$-blocked. \end{enumerate}

lean_code_with_comments:
/-- The walk `ŽĆ` is *$C$-$d$-blocked* (LN def 3.19, item 1):
either $v_0$ or $v_n$ lies in `C`, or some interior
non-collider position on `ŽĆ` lies in `C`, or some collider
position on `ŽĆ` lies outside $\Anc^G(C)$. -/
def IsDBlocked {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) : Prop :=
  (ŽĆ.nodeAt 0 Ōłł C) Ōł©
  (ŽĆ.nodeAt ŽĆ.length Ōłł C) Ōł©
  (Ōłā k, 0 < k Ōł¦ k < ŽĆ.length Ōł¦ ŽĆ.IsNonColliderAt k Ōł¦ ŽĆ.nodeAt k Ōłł C) Ōł©
  (Ōłā k, ŽĆ.IsColliderAt k Ōł¦ ŽĆ.nodeAt k Ōłē G.AncSet C)

/-- The walk `ŽĆ` is *$C$-$d$-open* (LN def 3.19, item 2):
the literal negation of `ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C`. -/
def IsDOpen {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) : Prop :=
  ┬¼ ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C

/-- Defining equation for `IsDOpen`. The LN-original
characterisation: "$\pi$ is $C$-$d$-open iff it is not
$C$-$d$-blocked". Holds by definition. -/
theorem isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked {v w : ╬▒}
    (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) :
    ŽĆ.IsDOpen C Ōåö ┬¼ ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C :=
  Iff.rfl
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsDBlocked {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) : Prop :=
  (ŽĆ.nodeAt 0 Ōłł C) Ōł©
  (ŽĆ.nodeAt ŽĆ.length Ōłł C) Ōł©
  (Ōłā k, 0 < k Ōł¦ k < ŽĆ.length Ōł¦ ŽĆ.IsNonColliderAt k Ōł¦ ŽĆ.nodeAt k Ōłł C) Ōł©
  (Ōłā k, ŽĆ.IsColliderAt k Ōł¦ ŽĆ.nodeAt k Ōłē G.AncSet C)

def IsDOpen {v w : ╬▒} (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) : Prop :=
  ┬¼ ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C

theorem isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked {v w : ╬▒}
    (ŽĆ : Walk G v w) (C : Set ╬▒) :
    ŽĆ.IsDOpen C Ōåö ┬¼ ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C :=
  Iff.rfl
lean_explanation:
The walk `ŽĆ : Walk G v w` runs between endpoints `v, w : ╬▒`, where `╬▒ : Type*` is the ambient vertex type. The graph `G : CDMG ╬▒` is a chain directed mixed graph (the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$); the conditioning set is `C : Set ╬▒`, with no subtype wrapper enforcing the LN's prose-level preamble $C \subseteq J \cup V$. Interior positions are indexed by `k : ŌäĢ` and bounded by `0 < k Ōł¦ k < ŽĆ.length`, picking out the genuinely interior positions of $\pi = (v_0, v_1, \ldots, v_n)$.

`ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C` is a `Prop`-valued four-way disjunction matching the LN's reading order. The two endpoint clauses use `ŽĆ.nodeAt 0 Ōłł C` and `ŽĆ.nodeAt ŽĆ.length Ōłł C` (the LN's $v_0 \in C$ and $v_n \in C$). The interior non-collider clause is an existential over positions $k$ satisfying both `ŽĆ.IsNonColliderAt k` ŌĆö which collapses the LN's left-chain, right-chain, and fork sub-patterns into a single non-collider predicate ŌĆö together with $v_k \in C$. The collider clause is an existential over positions $k$ with `ŽĆ.IsColliderAt k` and $v_k \notin G.\mathrm{AncSet}\ C$, Lean's name for the LN's $\Anc^G(C)$ supplied by the family-reachability module `Section3_1.FamilyReachability`.

Lean's dot-notation `ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C` works because `ŽĆ` is the first explicit argument, the same convention used throughout chapter 3 (`ŽĆ.IsColliderAt`, `ŽĆ.IsSigmaOpen`, and so on).

`ŽĆ.IsDOpen C` is the literal Lean negation `┬¼ ŽĆ.IsDBlocked C`; the LN itself defines $d$-open as 'not $d$-blocked', and the companion lemma `isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked` is consequently `Iff.rfl`.
design_choices:
**The four-disjunct shape mirrors the LN's reading order rather than the collapsed two-disjunct form.** A reader of the lecture notes sees clause (i) ŌĆö two endpoint sub-conditions ŌĆö followed by clause (ii) ŌĆö four sub-patterns (left chain, right chain, fork, collider). The first three sub-patterns of (ii) all read '$v_k \in C$' under a non-collider shape and together exhaust 'interior non-collider in $C$'; the fourth is the collider sub-clause. The Lean definition keeps this one-to-one correspondence: endpoint $v_0$, endpoint $v_n$, interior non-collider, collider. The natural alternative is a two-disjunct form ('some non-collider position is in $C$, or some collider position is not in $\Anc^G(C)$'), equivalent under the observation that endpoints are always non-colliders. We reject the collapse because the very next row (claim 3.28) is the LN's own statement of that equivalence; baking it into the definition would silently delete an articulated theorem.

**The interior bound `0 < k Ōł¦ k < ŽĆ.length` appears on the non-collider disjunct but not the collider disjunct.** With our `IsNonColliderAt`, endpoint positions are unconditionally non-colliders, so dropping the bound would let any walk with $v_0 \in C$ trigger the non-collider clause and collapse the four-disjunct shape. The collider predicate, by contrast, is already `False` at $k = 0$, $k = \pi.\mathrm{length}$, and at out-of-range positions, so any witness is automatically interior; a redundant bound would only burden downstream proofs with an extra discharge step.

**`IsNonColliderAt` rather than `IsBlockableNonColliderAt`.** This is the principal shape-level difference between $d$-blocking and $\sigma$-blocking (def 3.17). $\sigma$-blocking restricts attention to *blockable* non-colliders ŌĆö a sub-classification driven by the coreflexive-edge boundary of the CDMG ŌĆö and treats non-blockable non-colliders as '$\sigma$-open for free'. $d$-blocking imposes no such restriction: every non-collider position is a potential blocker. Using `IsBlockableNonColliderAt` here would silently identify def 3.19 with def 3.17, which is wrong even though under acyclicity (claim 3.20) the two collapse to the same family of walks.

**`IsDOpen` is the syntactic negation of `IsDBlocked`, not a parallel positive predicate.** The LN itself flips the asymmetry it used for $\sigma$-separation: $d$-open is defined in one line as 'not $d$-blocked'. We mirror that ŌĆö `IsDOpen := ┬¼ IsDBlocked` ŌĆö which makes `isDOpen_iff_not_isDBlocked` definitionally `Iff.rfl` and eliminates the classical-reasoning step the $\sigma$-version's De-Morgan dual requires. A caller wanting the positive conjunctive shape ('every endpoint not in $C$, every interior non-collider not in $C$, every collider in $\Anc^G(C)$') applies De Morgan on demand; we judged that cost worth paying to keep the LN's framing as the primitive shape.

**`C : Set ╬▒` rather than a `{ C // C ŌŖå G.J Ōł¬ G.V }` subtype.** The LN's preamble '$C \subseteq J \cup V$' is stated in prose, not enforced at the type level. `AncSet G C` already silently ignores members outside the graph, and threading a subtype through every downstream signature (def 3.20, the claim 3.28 / 29 / 30 chain, chapter $\ge 4$ Markov properties, do-calculus) would impose a real ergonomic cost for no proof benefit. Consumers who genuinely depend on the side-condition state it as a separate hypothesis at the call site.
